programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Britain's betting industry is booming, with the amount people gamble having almost doubled in a decade and company profits soaring to £14.5 billion. But are the bookies doing enough to protect problem gamblers? Reporter Bronagh Munro investigates an industry where complaints are rising even more quickly than profits, meeting one person who gambled away more than £3 million, and families who have lost their children to addiction.(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Oscar-winning actress Kate Winslet follows a rumour of Scandinavian ancestry on her late mother Sally's side of the family and is delighted when it turns out to be true and that she has Swedish heritage. However, her joy turns to tears and anger when she uncovers the extreme hardships her ancestors endured, from famine to flogging and imprisonment. Switching to her father's side of the family, Kate finds a drummer boy in the Grenadier Guards and, this time, an unusually positive encounter with prison(n)

programme.title: Fake Britain
programme.desc: Dom Littlewood and Michelle Ackerley with a report on the counterfeit contact lenses that can make your eyesight worse, and the fake waste disposal companies that could cost consumers a fortune. Elsewhere, Michelle shows how to identify fake emails and dangerous links online, and reveals how criminals can even embed fake messages into a legitimate text chain on your phone(n)

programme.title: Shop Well for Less?
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Steph McGovern are in Milton Keynes to meet the Howarth family - one of the biggest spending families to ever feature on the programme - to help them change their shopping habits in an attempt to save money. A lack of time management means the family has slid into some bad shopping habits, often buying two or three of the same household items, and having recently renovated their home, the family find themselves with credit card debt. Can Alex and Steph get this family to rein in their spending and get on top of their debt?(n)

programme.title: Sacred Wonders
programme.desc: What people do for faith in some of the most stunning sacred places on Earth. In the second episode, a Shinto devotee undertakes a gruelling challenge at the sacred Nachi Falls in Japan, hoping to ensure the wellbeing of his country for another year. Meanwhile in Mali, a young Muslim man helps to re-plaster Djenne's Great Mosque in the hope of a place in paradise, and at the Cathedral of St John the Divine in New York, an Anglican priest manages an astonishing ceremony to bring people - and their animals - closer to God(n)

programme.title: The Warwick Uni Rape Chat Scandal
programme.desc: Documentary about the scandal of online rape threats made by students in 2018, featuring contributions made from those involved in the case and examining the decision to allow some of the men involved back on campus, despite the original intention to bar them for 10 years(n)

programme.title: Serengeti
programme.desc: The aftermath of the devastating fire has left a blackened smoking wasteland, but rain soon brings hope to the scorched earth. Kike the cheetah and wild dog leader Jasari are both teaching their cubs how to survive on their own, only to be interrupted by a grieving giraffe. A baby elephant struggles in the flooded river, while Bakari returns when his family are threatened by a rival baboon troop. Lioness Kali has surprises in store which bring hope for the future of her pride, and as things draw to a close, a death brings all the families together in a remarkable truce(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: The Cruise: A Life at Sea
programme.desc: Documentary following life aboard the cruise ship Balmoral during its 112-day journey around the world, with 1,700 passengers and crew setting off from Southampton in January. A sick holidaymaker has to be airlifted off the ship in the middle of the Atlantic, while choir mistress Sue Alderson drills her volunteers for a performance of The Pirates of Penzance at the end of the leg in Acapulco(n)

programme.title: The Cruise: A Life at Sea
programme.desc: The Balmoral crosses the Pacific on the second leg of her round-the-world cruise, with a stop on the island of Tahiti along the way. Drama producer Carol Bunyan is hard at work with her volunteer thespians, Ian and Gail Hussey celebrate their 50th wedding anniversary by renewing their vows with the help of the Rev Colin Still, and there is tragedy when a passenger dies(n)

programme.title: Animal Park Summer Special
programme.desc: Ben Fogle and Kate Humble meet the newest diva at the Park - Inny the giant otter, while Jean Johansson discovers why koalas can sometimes hamper Australian detectives. Plus, the Park is placed on red alert as one of the carnivores goes missing(n)

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace visits a cherry Bakewell factory in Stoke on Trent that produces 250,000 tarts every day, and meets the team of 12 employed to precisely place the cherries on top by hand. Cherry Healey learns the tricks of avoiding a soggy pastry bottom when baking at home, and visits a factory where almonds are roasted and milled to make butter. Historian Ruth Goodman discovers the origins of frangipane as a perfume in 17th-century Paris, and discovers how the modern cherry Bakewell recipe was arrived at by accident(n)

programme.title: Curious Creatures
programme.desc: Kate Humble hosts the panel show that tests the wildlife knowledge of team captains Chris Packham and Lucy Cooke and their guests Christy Harrison and Niall Strawson(n)

programme.title: Getting the Builders In
programme.desc: Donna desperately needs an office revamp in Sandhurst and Karl has a £12,000 budget to overhaul his garden in Urmston, Greater Manchester(n)

programme.title: Len and Ainsley's Big Food Adventure
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott and Len Goodman head to Bradford, where the Strictly Come Dancing judge has his first taste of a Pakistani curry and samples some Latvian cuisine(n)

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Dave Myers and Si King explore the history behind the boom in pub food, tracing it from the humble beginnings of bar snacks and chicken-in-a-basket to the birth of the gastro pub. The duo also recreate some mouthwatering dishes that would make any publican proud(n)

programme.title: More Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: Vet Steve helps a kestrel with a damaged wing get back into the wild, while equine specialist Sybil treats a pony with a locked leg - and a reputation for misbehaviour. At the Small Animal Hospital in Fife, heart and lung expert Craig is under pressure as he attempts to save a Yorkshire terrier's life. Narrated by Felicity Kendal(n)

programme.title: Life Story
programme.desc: David Attenborough's documentary charting the circle of life in the animal kingdom ends with creatures' attempts to rear their offspring, revealing the commitment involved and how for some parents it means risking their own lives. A turtle hauls itself up the beach to lay its eggs in a safe place, only for the tide to trap it behind a wall of coral. A mother bonobo in the Congo teaches its son the skills it will need to survive adulthood, bison protect their calves from the ever-present threat of wolves and a mother zebra has to lead its foal across a dangerous river(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Experts Philip Serrell and Louise Gostelow set off on a search for items on a journey through East Anglia, heading for an auction showdown in Colchester(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: Musical comedian Mitch Benn, wine expert Joe Fattorini, broadcaster Samira Ahmed,sports presenter Steve Rider and writer Emma Kennedy take on the general-knowledge champions, hoping to win a cash prize for their chosen charity. Hosted by Jeremy Vine(n)

programme.title: The Customer Is Always Right
programme.desc: The consumers unbox, test and score a science kit, a pet seat carpet and a make-up cleanser and applicator. Lucy Alexander presents(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team restore four more treasured family heirlooms, tackling a musical wooden bench, a Victorian kaleidoscope, military items owned by a soldier who served in both world wars and a pair of Royal Crown Derby peacocks(n)

programme.title: Nadiya's Time to Eat
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain shares the recipes she relies on when she hasn't got the energy to cook anything complicated, including French toast spiced with turmeric, falafel burgers, and crispy apple pies. Nadiya also heads to the Heinz factory in Wigan to find out how the team meet the public's demand for baked beans, while she joins a busy vet during lambing season to help him reinvigorate his lunchtimes(n)

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: The fifth first-round match sees an Oxbridge clash as four students from Wolfson College, Cambridge pit their wits against a team from St John's College, Oxford in a bid to reach the second stage. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions(n)

programme.title: When Bridges Collapse: The Genoa Disaster
programme.desc: On August 14,2018, the Ponte Morandi in Genoa collapsed, sending 27 vehicles plummeting to the valley below with the loss of 43 lives. This investigation into the disaster tells the stories of people on the bridge that day and examines what caused the road viaduct to fail so catastrophically(n)

programme.title: I Love
programme.desc: The Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles look back over the year the high-kicking heroes in a half-shell took the world by storm, Bill and Ted set off on an Excellent Adventure and Debbie Gibson went head to head with Tiffany for pop supremacy. This Morning began dominating daytime television, Bros and Neighbours star Kylie Minogue hit the charts, and the emergence of acid house changed the clubbing scene(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emma Barnett(n)

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: No Details Available(n)

programme.title: Tribes, Predators & Me
programme.desc: Wildlife cameraman Gordon Buchanan travels across Ethiopia to find out how people can live so close to hyenas, powerful carnivores that weigh up to 14 stone and are capable of killing lions in packs. He starts his journey in the capital Addis Ababa, where hyenas prowl the outskirts for food and are tolerated because people believe they ward off the devil. He also meets cattle-herders who dislike hyenas because of attacks on their livestock, and visits the medieval city of Harar, which has a reputation for having hundreds of hyenas, many of which inhabit the local graveyard(n)

programme.title: Fosse/Verdon
programme.desc: The early 1970s finds Bob focused on editing the musical movie Cabaret. Gwen, meanwhile, auditions for the leading role in a straight play that forces her to reflect on the beginning of her career, her disastrous first marriage to James Henaghan and a career change that led to her breakthrough performance in Can-Can. Sam Rockwell and Michelle Williams star in this biographical drama detailing the lives of choreographer-director Bob Fosse and celebrated Broadway dancer Gwen Verdon. With Norbert Leo Butz and Peter Scolari(n)

programme.title: Serengeti
programme.desc: The dry season is now upon the animals - a tough time for some, but for others a food bonanza. Jasari and his wild dog family are under siege from the hyena clan, so he must train his pups to survive, while Bakari the baboon is living in exile from the troop, only to spy the leader bullying his adopted baby. Kali and her cubs are enjoying life back in the heart of the pride, only to be attacked by the black-maned lions. But worse is to come when a storm ignites a devastating fire, consuming the dry land with flames(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: The team heads to Norfolk, where John Craven meets the team behind a corncrake breeding programme, while Margherita Taylor meets artist Shauna Richardson and a sheepdog trainer who also breeds red squirrels. Tom Heap looks at how devices designed to scare seals away from salmon stocks could be having unintended consequences and Adam Henson is in Kent to meets a young shepherdess making a name for herself in the sheepdog handling world(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: The Cruise: A Life at Sea
programme.desc: The Balmoral travels from Indonesia to Singapore on the next leg of its round-the-world trip, and the Rev Colin Still gets a reminder of his past on a steam train trip in Java. Choir mistress Sue Alderson starts to drill a new group of singers to perform at Colin's Palm Sunday service, but a change of schedule in Singapore causes her problems(n)

programme.title: The Cruise: A Life at Sea
programme.desc: It's Easter, and the Rev Colin Still has a busy week ahead, including attending a Mission to Seafarers service in Colombo, Sri Lanka. The Balmoral also stops in Phuket, Thailand, where Colin visits a Buddhist temple and choir mistress Sue Alderson joins him for an elephant ride at a sanctuary. Ken and Shirley Marshall are celebrating their 60th wedding anniversary on the cruise and experience an exhilarating journey on a motorised rickshaw in Colombo(n)

programme.title: Animal Park Summer Special
programme.desc: Jean Johansson finds out how Longleat's giant otters Gordito and Inny are getting on, while Kate Humble helps put the European grey wolves' stamina to the test(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo heads from Warwick to Rye in East Sussex. In the orchards of East Malling, Kent, Michael discovers how the Edwardians' serious attitude towards cultivation bears fruit even now. In Folkestone, he hears how the town coped with an influx of more than 100,000 refugees fleeing the German invasion in 1914. In the High Weald, Michael heads for Tenterden Town and the light railway that opened in 1900. His final stop is the medieval Cinque Port of Rye, where he tours the home and garden of author Henry James(n)

programme.title: Our Lives
programme.sub-title: Taking on the Irish Sea - Our Lives
programme.desc: Following the 12-strong crew from Llangrannog rowing club in Ceredigion as they prepare and compete in sea-rowing race the Celtic Challenge across the Irish Sea from Arklow in the Republic of Ireland to Aberystwyth. The eight women and four men, including three grandmothers, a fire officer, a police inspector, a builder, a veterinary nurse, a foster carer and a student, all pull together for nearly 100 miles of open sea that can take over a day to cross. Dot Davies narrates(n)

programme.title: Curious Creatures

programme.title: Len and Ainsley's Big Food Adventure
programme.desc: The pair reach Leicester, where Len Goodman braves his first ever Indian curry and samples a selection of Greek cuisine. Chef Ainsley Harriott heads to the kitchen to create spiced fish cakes, inspired by the day's adventures in the city(n)

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers celebrate quality produce that needs to be cooked with the respect it deserves, as they continue their culinary journey through time. The duo argue that, while convenience food serves a purpose, only the best ingredients and perfect cooking can produce something truly delicious(n)

programme.title: More Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: Richard is called to the scene when a calf's mother collapses, while Sybil has to break some difficult news to a concerned horse owner. Plus, vets in Fife treat puppies that have eaten a potentially deadly amount of ibuprofen, and Megan deals with a chinchilla with problematic teeth. Narrated by Felicity Kendal(n)

programme.title: Extraordinary Animals
programme.desc: Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall and a team of wildlife experts travel the globe in search of the most surprising animal stories and reveal how and why animals do such remarkable things. The first episode explores the world of animal intelligence, with Hugh visiting Florida to investigate an unexpected relationship between an alligator and a manatee, which has animal intelligence experts baffled. Plus, conservationist Giles Clark finds out if elephants can feel empathy - one of the most sophisticated forms of emotional intelligence(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Philip Serrell and Louise Gostelow search for antiques in Essex and Suffolk, where newcomer Louise gambles every penny of her kitty in a valiant effort to unseat her rival(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: Actor and writer George Costigan, authors Clare Mackintosh and Wendy Holden, illustrator Nick Sharratt and poet Caleb Femi take on the general-knowledge champions(n)

programme.title: The Customer Is Always Right
programme.desc: Consumers offer their views on a backpack that can be twisted round to the front for easy access, a rotating wrist torch, and a button-making set that requires no glue, scissors or needles. With Lucy Alexander(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Dominic Chinea repairs a memorial to First World War soldiers from the village of Norton, and a woman seeks help restoring a teddy bear that belonged to her late stepfather. Plus, Geoff Harvey gets to work on a German-built fruit machine and silversmith Brenton West takes on the repair of a precious solid silver trophy presented by the Royal Navy(n)

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace visits a croissant factory in France that produces 336,000 pastries a day, and discovers how an 83-year-old strain of yeast is used to create the perfect flavour. Cherry Healey talks to a professor who specialises in exploring human senses about the perfect way to eat a croissant and visits a farm that produces concentrated butter that is 99.8 percent fat. Plus, Ruth Goodman is in Paris investigating the croissant's surprising Austrian origins and the role of bread in the French revolution(n)

programme.title: The Chefs' Brigade
programme.desc: Jason Atherton takes the chefs to Cadiz in southern Spain, a city famous for its seafood. The brigade must cook for a restaurant that has been run by the same family for 50 years, and is adored by locals and tourists alike. Jason must encourage the chefs to appreciate local traditions, and arranges for a local fisherman to take them to the salt flats that have provided seafood to residents for thousands of years. Back in the kitchen, several members of the brigade have to win back Jason's trust following recent confrontations(n)

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Dara O Briain looks back on the latest events in news and politics, with the help of Angela Barnes, Ed Byrne, Rhys James, Nish Kumar, Ellie Taylor and regular panellist Hugh Dennis. Yet again, stand-up and improvisation are the name of the game as the contestants try to get one up on one another(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Kirsty Wark(n)

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: No Details Available(n)

programme.title: The Misadventures of Romesh Ranganathan
programme.desc: Romesh Ranganathan visits Bosnia and Herzegovina to discover how this small corner of Eastern Europe has changed since the war, with the aid of a local museum curator. He tries out Sarajevo's abandoned bobsleigh track, a remnant of the 1984 winter Olympics, stays in a 'war hostel', and meets a man who claims to have discovered the world's largest pyramid(n)

programme.title: Broke
programme.desc: Documentary following more people who are in work but struggling to make ends meet. They include Tyrone, a homeless teenager who works a zero-hour contract at McDonald's and wants to rent his own flat - but cannot guarantee he will earn enough each week to cover the rent. Plus, the story of Ecuadorian immigrant Angelica, who lives in London, where she studies English all day and works night shifts as a cleaner. After requesting a pay rise to the living wage, she was suspended by her employment agency(n)

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family: Case Closed?
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Jeremy Dein and Sasha Wass look back at their investigation of a rural case of murder and moonlighting in Ireland in 1894, when James Donovan was dragged from his bed and beaten to death by two men(n)

programme.title: One Day That Changed My Life
programme.desc: Return of the programme following people going through pivotal experiences. After developing multiple sclerosis, ex-railway worker Simon finds out if a dog can restore his independence, while entrepreneurs Charlotte and Kim risk their business in a bid to launch a new product. Dominic Littlewood narrates(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: A to Z of TV Gardening
programme.desc: Carol Kirkwood's alphabetical journey through the world of horticulture continues with the letter M. Various BBC gardening presenters are mesmerised by magnolias, marvel at the monkey puzzle tree and meet a marigold enthusiast(n)

programme.title: The Cruise: A Life at Sea
programme.desc: The Balmoral leaves Dubai as it sets off on the last leg of its world tour, stopping briefly in Oman before heading through the Suez Canal to the Egyptian port of Alexandria. The Rev Colin Still and a group of passengers visit the Allied war cemetery at El Alamein to commemorate those that fell in the battle. On board, people come together to take part in the Country Fayre to raise money for charity, and then it's time for end-of-cruise shows by the passengers and staff, who bid tearful goodbyes at the end of the four-month voyage(n)

programme.title: Animal Park Summer Special
programme.desc: Ben Fogle and Kate Humble meet two endangered Cuban crocodiles, while keepers and vets are battling to get to the bottom of why rare a red panda is not putting on weight(n)

programme.title: Animal Babies: First Year on Earth
programme.desc: Cameras follow six baby animals as they learn the basic skills needed to face the challenges of surviving their first year on Earth. In Kenya, new-born elephant calf Safina learns how to keep up with her fast moving herd, and in Sri Lanka, one-week-old Jazir has to quickly learn the ropes of a primate society. At the edge of the Arctic, five-week-old fox cubs must learn to hunt before their siblings beat them to limited food, and in Uganda, an eight-week-old mountain gorilla adapts to forest life(n)

programme.title: BBC News at 9
programme.desc: No Details Available(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Curious Creatures
programme.desc: Kate Humble hosts, while team captains Chris Packham and Lucy Cooke are joined by Anita Rani and Steve Leonard for another test of wildlife knowledge(n)

programme.title: Getting the Builders In
programme.desc: The first build on offer is in Stretford, where Karen wants to convert her cellar into a playroom for her train-obsessed stepson on a budget of £1,500. In Woking, Ross and Giovanna's bedroom hasn't been updated in decades and is need of a complete refit, including an entire wall of bespoke wardrobes(n)

programme.title: Len and Ainsley's Big Food Adventure
programme.desc: Len Goodman has his first taste of Korean barbecue food and samples Swedish cuisine, and Ainsley Harriott cooks a salmon and mango salad inspired by the day's adventures(n)

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explore the nation's love affair with beer and cider, revealing how at one time it was safer to drink ale than water. The duo are also given a chance to gain hands-on brewing experience at a cider festival(n)

programme.title: More Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: Vet Rosie treats a concussed tawny owl, while equine specialist Kerry helps a horse with a badly cut face. Meanwhile, in Fife, Adam has an emotional case on his hands when an owner suffering from a debilitating phobia discovers the dog he depends on has a worrying lump. Narrated by Felicity Kendal(n)

programme.title: Extraordinary Animals
programme.desc: Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall and a team of wildlife experts focus on animals' relationships with each other and with humans. Hugh discovers how the capuchin monkeys of Costa Rica have such a fantastic team spirit, biologist Patrick Aryee investigates man's relationship with the vulture - nature's most infamous scavenger - and zoologist Lucy Cooke finds out about the medicine in Kentucky that may improve a rare pig's relationship problems. Conservationist Giles Clark ventures into the rainforests of Thailand to find out how a clever matchmaking technique could help save the clouded leopard from extinction(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Experts Phil Serrell and Louise Gostelow head to Lincolnshire to search for antiques, where they come across an antique globe and a very rare King Charles Spaniel(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: Former Blue Peter presenter Zoe Salmon, medical expert Dr Sara Kayat, comedian Shazia Mirza, former Team GB runner Iwan Thomas and star of Channel 4's The Hotel Mark Jenkins set out to win a cash prize for their charity. Hosted by Jeremy Vine(n)

programme.title: The Customer Is Always Right
programme.desc: Customers unbox, test and score a mirror and indicator for cyclists, healthy baking mixes and a 3D noticeboard. But only one can win the Customer is Always Right trophy(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring four treasured family heirlooms back to life. Ceramics conservator Kirsten Ramsay is charged with the task of repairing a little cat teapot with an extraordinary wartime story behind it, while Dominic Chinea takes on the restoration of a once brightly coloured wooden carousel horse. The team also brings a wooden boat puzzle and a Victorian magic lantern back to life(n)

programme.title: Interior Design Masters
programme.desc: Aspiring designers take on commercial interior challenges, as they compete to win a life changing contract. Presented by Fearne Cotton. In the first episode, the ten contestants are split into two teams and must redesign two show homes, working together to deliver a cohesive end product that impresses the judges and is accessible to prospective buyers(n)

programme.title: Murder Case
programme.desc: With access to Police Scotland's Major Investigations Teams (MITs), this series goes behind the scenes on the most complex murder and large-scale criminal investigations. This episode follows the investigation into the disappearance and subsequent murder of 47-year-old Julie Reilly, who went missing from her Govan flat in March 2018(n)

programme.title: Better Things
programme.desc: Phyllis is behaving more eratically and becoming more confused when she is hospitalised. Contains some strong language. Comedy drama, starring Pamela Adlon(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: The chef visits Mercato Delle Erbe, which is a vegetable market, and eats squid stuffed with potato and capers(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emma Barnett(n)

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: No Details Available(n)

programme.title: The Chefs' Brigade
programme.desc: Jason Atherton takes the chefs to Cadiz in Southern Spain, a city famous for its seafood. The brigade must cook for a restaurant that has been run by the same family for 50 years, and is adored by locals and tourists alike. Jason must encourage the chefs to appreciate local traditions, and arranges for a local fisherman to take them to the salt flats that have provided seafood to residents for thousands of years. Back in the kitchen, several members of the brigade have to win back Jason's trust following recent confrontations(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: Iolo's Snowdonia
programme.desc: Iolo Williams explores Snowdonia through the seasons, beginning during spring in Nant Ffrancon in the Ogwen Valley. A mountain guide takes him high up above the glacial landscape of Cwm Idwal, before the wildlife expert heads to a hidden river gorge in full flow, listens to the dawn chorus in an ancient woodland, and visits Bird Rock, an imposing crag in the Dysynni Valley(n)

programme.title: Operation Grand Canyon with Dan Snow
programme.desc: Part two of two. The historian and his team continue down the Colorado River in antique wooden boats as they re-create the pioneering 280-mile expedition through the Grand Canyon that was led by explorer John Wesley Powell in 1869. Along the way they carry out the same surveying tasks as the original party with period equipment, including taking photographs with a 19th-century 3D camera(n)

programme.title: Deep Ocean
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates a documentary following a crew attempting to film bioluminescent creatures in a huge underwater canyon in California's Monterey Bay(n)

programme.title: Life
programme.desc: David Attenborough turns his attention to marine invertebrates - bizarre and beautiful animals that thrive in the toughest parts of the ocean. Divers swim into a shoal of predatory Humboldt squid emerging from the depths to hunt in packs, and timelapse photography reveals starfish gathering under the Arctic ice to devour a seal carcass. A camera also follows a female octopus into a cave, where she hides to protect her eggs until she starves to death(n)

programme.title: Farther and Sun: A Dyslexic Road Trip
programme.desc: Film-maker Richard Macer examines the condition with his dyslexic son, prior to being tested for the condition himself. Their journey brings them into contact with successful dyslexic people, including Richard Branson and Eddie Izzard, uncovers the challenges dyslexic people face in life, and visit companies that are actively seeking dyslexic staff, viewing the condition as a potential advantage(n)

programme.title: How the Celts Saved Britain
programme.desc: Part one of two. Dan Snow unravels the mystery of the lost years of 400-800AD, looking at the demise of civilisation in Britain following the collapse of the Roman Empire, and revealing how Christianity transformed Ireland into a cultural powerhouse(n)

programme.title: My Astonishing Self: Gabriel Byrne on George Bernard Shaw
programme.desc: The actor explores the life, works and passions of the playwright, examining Shaw's radical and unapologetic political thinking and ability to charm and satirise the establishment(n)

programme.title: The Art of Spain
programme.desc: Andrew Graham-Dixon concludes his journey in northern Spain, where modern art by masters such as Pablo Picasso, Joan Miro and Salvador Dali flourished in the 20th century. These visionaries produced work that pushed the boundaries of the discipline, while also reflecting the tumultuous social and political events of the century, culminating in the rise and fall of fascism under General Franco(n)

programme.title: Africa: A Journey into Music
programme.desc: Rita Ray travels to Mali in west Africa, home to a deep musical culture and ancient instruments that are the hallmark of their sound. Mali has produced more Grammy-winning artists than any other African country and this well of talent has drawn in artists and producers from around the world to collaborate with its musicians. While the country has been rocked by Islamist insurgency leading to a ban on music in some areas, Rita finds a traditional way of life and rich musical culture has endured(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: Iolo's Snowdonia
programme.desc: Iolo Williams explores the area around the summit of Snowdon in summer, finding buzzards nesting and moths being hunted by migrant nightjars from Africa. He also climbs Cadair Idris, the highest peak in the south part of the park, visits sand dunes full of orchids and spends a day on the moors watching the hen harrier.(n)

programme.title: SAS: Rogue Warriors
programme.desc: Ben Macintyre concludes his history of formation of the British Army special forces unit. With David Stirling locked away in Hitler's most secure prison Colditz, the leadership of the SAS passed to Paddy Mayne, a man who built his reputation on the battlefield as a warrior of the first rank. In 1943, the SAS leaves the desert for Europe to enter a darker and far more complex theatre of war, where they face the threat of execution and the trauma of civilian casualties, as well as being the first to witness the nightmare of Bergen-Belsen concentration camp(n)

programme.title: Revolutions: The Ideas That Changed the World
programme.desc: Jim Al-Khalili presents a look at the history behind the smartphone, discovering how the Roman army sent messages over long distances using fire and examining the advances made by Samuel Morse, Alexander Graham Bell's favour and Guglielmo Marconi. The programme also examines how the inventions of scientist and movie star Hedy Lamarr has been used to increase network capacity(n)

programme.title: The Rise and Fall of Nokia Mobile
programme.desc: How the Finnish mobile phone company's global domination was created and then eroded, told by those that lived and worked through the rollercoaster years at Nokia(n)

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: Investigating the story of how a Russian internet millionaire, Dmitry Itskov, is turning to cutting-edge science to try to unlock the secret of living for ever. The programme investigates the real science inspiring his bold plan to upload the human mind to a computer, and examines whether his goal of bringing about immortality for humans within thirty years is attainable(n)

programme.title: The Somme: Secret Tunnel Wars
programme.desc: Documentary following historian Peter Barton and a team of archaeologists as they navigate a network of tunnels constructed beneath the Somme battlefield during the First World War. The programme unearths the history embedded in the underground labyrinth - facilitated by both the Allied and Central Powers to plant mines beyond enemy lines - and the personal stories of the predominantly British troops who helped dig it during the first two years of the conflict(n)

programme.title: Battlefield Britain
programme.desc: The Snows use state-of-the-art graphics to re-create the Battle of Britain, one of the pivotal conflicts of the Second World War. Peter explains the role of radar in detecting Luftwaffe planes as they headed for Dover, while Dan experiences the physiological effects of the high G-forces that would have been felt by the RAF pilots(n)

programme.title: SAS: Rogue Warriors
programme.desc: Ben Macintyre concludes his history of formation of the British Army special forces unit. With David Stirling locked away in Hitler's most secure prison Colditz, the leadership of the SAS passed to Paddy Mayne, a man who built his reputation on the battlefield as a warrior of the first rank. In 1943, the SAS leaves the desert for Europe to enter a darker and far more complex theatre of war, where they face the threat of execution and the trauma of civilian casualties, as well as being the first to witness the nightmare of Bergen-Belsen concentration camp(n)

programme.title: Revolutions: The Ideas That Changed the World
programme.desc: Jim Al-Khalili presents a look at the history behind the smartphone, discovering how the Roman army sent messages over long distances using fire and examining the advances made by Samuel Morse, Alexander Graham Bell's favour and Guglielmo Marconi. The programme also examines how the inventions of scientist and movie star Hedy Lamarr has been used to increase network capacity(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: Eisteddfod 2019 with Jason Mohammad
programme.desc: Part one of two. The presenter reports from Llanrwst in the Conwy Valley, bringing highlights and stories from the National Eisteddfod of Wales. He catches up with Welsh rugby stars Jonathan Davies and Ken Owens, as well as broadcaster and comedian Tudur Owen, who are all being honoured at the Eisteddfod this year(n)

programme.title: A History of Scotland
programme.desc: Historian Neil Oliver explores the roles played by Alexander II, who forged the country through blood and violence, and William Wallace, a patriot whose resistance to King Edward of England inspired Scottish people to fight for their independence(n)

programme.title: The First Georgians: The German Kings Who Made Britain
programme.desc: Lucy Worsley investigates how George II's family fought the French, the Jacobites and one another at the same time. She examines his troubles with his son Frederick, Prince of Wales, and the way he led from the front on the battlefield, becoming the last British monarch to do so, and helped turn the country into a global superpower(n)

programme.title: Mary Beard's Ultimate Rome: Empire Without Limit
programme.desc: Scholar Mary Beard tackles one of the biggest puzzles of all as she examines how and when the Roman Empire fell, surveying the walls and fortifications of Britain and Germany. She seeks to redefine understanding of so-called 'Barbarian Invasions', before ultimately asking whether the Empire was transformed rather than destroyed, and still lives on in the world today(n)

programme.title: Susan Calman's Fringe Benefits
programme.desc: The comedian introduces highlights from the Edinburgh Fringe, showcasing some of this year's best new talent. She chats to special guests including Frank Skinner, Larry Dean, Iain Stirling and Desiree Burch, and introduces performances by Jo Caulfield and Stuart Mitchell(n)

programme.title: Timeshift
programme.sub-title: Timeshift: The Last Days of Steam
programme.desc: Between 1948 and 1960, governments around the world invested in electric tracks and diesel, but in Britain hundreds of steam-powered trains were built. This film looks at the reasons behind their continued production, and asks what happened to them(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio. Presented by Matt Baker and Michelle Ackerley(n)

programme.title: Fake Britain
programme.desc: Dom Littlewood and Michelle Ackerley with a report on the counterfeit contact lenses that can make your eyesight worse, and the fake waste disposal companies that could cost consumers a fortune. Elsewhere, Michelle shows how to identify fake emails and dangerous links online, and reveals how criminals can even embed fake messages into a legitimate text chain on your phone(n)

programme.title: Shop Well for Less?
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Steph McGovern are in Milton Keynes to meet the Howarth family - one of the biggest spending families to ever feature on the programme - to help them change their shopping habits in an attempt to save money. A lack of time management means the family has slid into some bad shopping habits, often buying two or three of the same household items, and having recently renovated their home, the family find themselves with credit card debt. Can Alex and Steph get this family to rein in their spending and get on top of their debt?(n)

programme.title: Sacred Wonders
programme.desc: What people do for faith in some of the most stunning sacred places on Earth. In the second episode, a Shinto devotee undertakes a gruelling challenge at the sacred Nachi Falls in Japan, hoping to ensure the wellbeing of his country for another year. Meanwhile in Mali, a young Muslim man helps to re-plaster Djenne's Great Mosque in the hope of a place in paradise, and at the Cathedral of St John the Divine in New York, an Anglican priest manages an astonishing ceremony to bring people - and their animals - closer to God(n)

programme.desc: No Details Available(n)

programme.title: The Warwick Uni Rape Chat Scandal
programme.desc: Documentary about the scandal of online rape threats made by students in 2018, featuring contributions made from those involved in the case and examining the decision to allow some of the men involved back on campus, despite the original intention to bar them for 10 years(n)

programme.title: Saving Britain's Worst Zoo
programme.desc: The documentary about the Tweedys' zoo nightmare picks up as news breaks of the shooting of the escaped lynx and the accidental death of another one. With debts in excess of £300,000 and their story making international news, they now face online death threats and calls for their zoo to be closed, while wildlife experts condemn the family and the council. The family face financial ruin and court proceedings. Can things possibly get any worse?(n)

programme.title: Serengeti
programme.desc: The aftermath of the devastating fire has left a blackened smoking wasteland, but rain soon brings hope to the scorched earth. Kike the cheetah and wild dog leader Jasari are both teaching their cubs how to survive on their own, only to be interrupted by a grieving giraffe. A baby elephant struggles in the flooded river, while Bakari returns when his family are threatened by a rival baboon troop. Lioness Kali has surprises in store which bring hope for the future of her pride, and as things draw to a close, a death brings all the families together in a remarkable truce(n)

programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Presented by Dan Walker and Louise Minchin(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley helps a house-hunting couple with a £900,000 budget move back from France and find an impressive rural retreat in the Norfolk countryside. Whilst in the county, Ginny heads to the Norfolk Broads to discover the important role that mills have played in keeping this low lying landscape above water. She visits the Wind Power Museum, home to a collection of mills and engineering gems, to hear the story of one local mill restoration hero(n)

programme.title: Our Lives
programme.desc: Documentary following Ned, Sophie, Rowan and Sam Scharer as they take over as the wardens of Bardsey Island off the coast of off the Llyn Peninsula in Gwynedd, managing, renovating and caring for the island while hoping to become closer as a family in the process(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: An unknown lady arrives looking for Callum with heartbreaking news, while the Ahmeds' Eid celebrations get off to a bad start when Habiba, Iqra and Adam struggle to remain civil. Kat comes clean to Kush about why Stacey and Martin fled, and Stuart attempts to make amends with a reluctant Rainie(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Britain's betting industry is booming, with the amount people gamble having almost doubled in a decade and company profits soaring to £14.5 billion. But are the bookies doing enough to protect problem gamblers? Reporter Bronagh Munro investigates an industry where complaints are rising even more quickly than profits, meeting one person who gambled away more than £3 million, and families who have lost their children to addiction.(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Oscar-winning actress Kate Winslet follows a rumour of Scandinavian ancestry on her late mother Sally's side of the family and is delighted when it turns out to be true and that she has Swedish heritage. However, her joy turns to tears and anger when she uncovers the extreme hardships her ancestors endured, from famine to flogging and imprisonment. Switching to her father's side of the family, Kate finds a drummer boy in the Grenadier Guards and, this time, an unusually positive encounter with prison(n)

programme.title: River City

programme.title: Keeping Faith
programme.desc: Evan's release from prison draws ever closer, and the return of her husband brings conflicting emotions for Faith and her family. The lawyer struggles with the demands of her growing feelings for Steve and doing what is best for her children. DI Breeze continues to pile the pressure on Evan as he awaits his freedom, and a phone call from PC Williams delivers an unwelcome new lead in Madlen's case. The Wales-set legal drama continues. Starring Eve Myles, Bradley Freegard, Rhashan Stone and Eiry Thomas(n)

programme.title: Reggie Yates's Extreme UK
programme.sub-title: Reggie Yates's Extreme UK: Men at War
programme.desc: Reggie meets the angry young British men who think feminism has gone too far, inlcuding those espousing men's rights at Speakers' Corner in Hyde Park, an underground online movement of male separatists and self-styled pick-up artist Roosh V, who dishes out advice on how to have more sex with women. Reggie quickly discovers how dark this world can be - from the men trolling feminists online to the influential agitators advising how to change a woman's mind when she says 'no'(n)

programme.desc: Callum struggles to focus at work and has a confrontation with Whitney, who demands to know what is going on, while Mariam and Arshad scold Iqra for not being honest with them. With Ian struggling to process Bobby's news, Kathy gives him a stern reality check, leading Ian to suggest him and Bobby sit down and talk over dinner(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Chloe desperately tries to convince Evan that their marriage is over as he bombards her with calls, photos and flowers and refuses to take no for an answer. Hanssen is forced to choose between work and family as he prepares for an important operation, and Dominic and Lofty are surprised by the outcome of their relationship counselling, but secrets threaten to tear them apart(n)

programme.title: Keeping Faith
programme.desc: Evan's release from prison draws ever closer, and the return of her husband brings conflicting emotions for Faith and her family. The lawyer struggles with the demands of her growing feelings for Steve and doing what is best for her children. DI Breeze continues to pile the pressure on Evan as he awaits his freedom, and a phone call from PC Williams delivers an unwelcome new lead in Madlen's case. The Wales-set legal drama continues. Starring Eve Myles, Bradley Freegard, Rhashan Stone and Eiry Thomas(n)

programme.title: Reggie Yates's Extreme UK
programme.sub-title: Reggie Yates's Extreme UK: Men at War
programme.desc: Reggie meets the angry young British men who think feminism has gone too far, inlcuding those espousing men's rights at Speakers' Corner in Hyde Park, an underground online movement of male separatists and self-styled pick-up artist Roosh V, who dishes out advice on how to have more sex with women. Reggie quickly discovers how dark this world can be - from the men trolling feminists online to the influential agitators advising how to change a woman's mind when she says 'no'(n)

programme.title: Fake Britain
programme.desc: Dom Littlewood and Michelle Ackerley with a report on the counterfeit contact lenses that can make your eyesight worse, and the fake waste disposal companies that could cost consumers a fortune. Elsewhere, Michelle shows how to identify fake emails and dangerous links online, and reveals how criminals can even embed fake messages into a legitimate text chain on your phone(n)

programme.title: Shop Well for Less?
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Steph McGovern are in Milton Keynes to meet the Howarth family - one of the biggest spending families to ever feature on the programme - to help them change their shopping habits in an attempt to save money. A lack of time management means the family has slid into some bad shopping habits, often buying two or three of the same household items, and having recently renovated their home, the family find themselves with credit card debt. Can Alex and Steph get this family to rein in their spending and get on top of their debt?(n)

programme.title: Sacred Wonders
programme.desc: What people do for faith in some of the most stunning sacred places on Earth. In the second episode, a Shinto devotee undertakes a gruelling challenge at the sacred Nachi Falls in Japan, hoping to ensure the wellbeing of his country for another year. Meanwhile in Mali, a young Muslim man helps to re-plaster Djenne's Great Mosque in the hope of a place in paradise, and at the Cathedral of St John the Divine in New York, an Anglican priest manages an astonishing ceremony to bring people - and their animals - closer to God(n)

programme.title: The Warwick Uni Rape Chat Scandal
programme.desc: Documentary about the scandal of online rape threats made by students in 2018, featuring contributions made from those involved in the case and examining the decision to allow some of the men involved back on campus, despite the original intention to bar them for 10 years(n)

programme.title: Serengeti
programme.desc: The aftermath of the devastating fire has left a blackened smoking wasteland, but rain soon brings hope to the scorched earth. Kike the cheetah and wild dog leader Jasari are both teaching their cubs how to survive on their own, only to be interrupted by a grieving giraffe. A baby elephant struggles in the flooded river, while Bakari returns when his family are threatened by a rival baboon troop. Lioness Kali has surprises in store which bring hope for the future of her pride, and as things draw to a close, a death brings all the families together in a remarkable truce(n)

programme.title: The Reith Lectures
programme.desc: Jonathan Sumption assesses the US and UK's constitutional models. He describes Britain's unwritten constitution as a political institution. programme.desc: The broadcaster meets Florida woman Jennifer Mee who, as a teenager, became a media sensation when she suffered a particularly strange ailment - a persistent case of hiccups. Even though she didn't pull the trigger, and was nowhere near when the bullets were fired, Jennifer was sentenced to life without parole. Piers charts the journey through fame and rejection that saw Jennifer descend into a criminal lifestyle, and asks family members and legal experts if they believe her punishment was too harsh(n)

Plus, local business owners share their thoughts on the event. While some stand to make a profit from the visitors, others find the size of the fair overwhelming(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed

Edinburgh guest-house manager Tony Costa is not happy to find dog hairs on his bed, and Graham Neil is unimpressed after taking a dip in the hot tub. programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Chester's Penguin Island is home to one of the most enduring love stories in the zoo. At 22, Rud is the oldest on the island, and has been with his partner, Spike, for over 15 years. However, keepers are concerned that Rud's arthritic hip could be affecting his quality of life. Meanwhile, the Sumatran tigers are upgrading to a luxury new enclosure on the other side of the zoo as part of Chester's huge expansion. But they've lived in their home for most of their lives so it's not going to be an easy move(n)

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Professor Noel Fitzpatrick and his team return for an eleventh series. In the first episode, a tiny Jackahuahua puppy is in need of emergency spinal surgery after being attacked by a dog in the park, but it is not certain that he will be able to get back on his feet again after the operation. Elsewhere, a Bernese has a rare cyst in his elbow, requiring a challenging operation for removal, and an excitable Labrador requires elbow replacement surgery, which will result in a long and difficult road to recovery(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Series following a British family restoring a French chateau. As summer arrives, Dick Strawbridge and Angel Adoree are busier than ever with seven weddings and 14 food lovers' weekends to prepare for. And as if that wasn't enough to keep them busy, they must also sort out the garden, work on a stables conversion and embark on a surprising floating adventure(n)

programme.title: Great Canal Journeys
programme.desc: Prunella Scales and Timothy West embark on a final canal journey in the first of two programmes exploring Vietnam and Cambodia. The trip begins in the limestone islands of Halong Bay, before travelling along the Red River to Hanoi and the ancient citadel of Hoa Lu. They also attend the famous lantern festival in the trading port of Hoi An(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A 77-year-old former motorbike racer arrives with a potentially deadly swelling of the main artery that could burst at any moment. His only option is major surgery, which could be dangerous at his age. A woman whose boyfriend was killed in a cycling accident a year previously is also injured while on her bike, and a medical technician and a mobile caterer talk about their roles in aiding people to recovery(n)

programme.title: Fatberg Autopsy: Secrets of the Sewers
programme.desc: Under the streets of Britain's cities an epidemic of fatbergs plagues the sewers. These monster blockages of congealed fat and human waste are a product of what people have flushed down our toilets and sinks. Presenter Rick Edwards joins a team of scientists and sewer workers to perform the first fatberg autopsy - taken from the biggest blockage ever found in the UK, at east London's Abbey Mills Pumping Station. Pathology technician Carla Valentine and experts analyse the contents to discover the chemistry, biology and toxicology of this beast from the sewer(n)

programme.title: Shocking Emergency Calls
programme.desc: A family trip to the Cincinnati Zoo turns into chaos and horror as a three-year-old falls into the enclosure of Harambe, the silverback gorilla(n)

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Professor Noel Fitzpatrick and his team return for an eleventh series. In the first episode, a tiny Jackahuahua puppy is in need of emergency spinal surgery after being attacked by a dog in the park, but it is not certain that he will be able to get back on his feet again after the operation. Elsewhere, a Bernese has a rare cyst in his elbow, requiring a challenging operation for removal, and an excitable Labrador requires elbow replacement surgery, which will result in a long and difficult road to recovery(n)

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Chester's Penguin Island is home to one of the most enduring love stories in the zoo. At 22, Rud is the oldest on the island, and has been with his partner, Spike, for over 15 years. However, keepers are concerned that Rud's arthritic hip could be affecting his quality of life. Meanwhile, the Sumatran tigers are upgrading to a luxury new enclosure on the other side of the zoo as part of Chester's huge expansion. But they've lived in their home for most of their lives so it's not going to be an easy move(n)

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty finds out just how baked our baked beans are in the US. Kate Quilton asks why stinky cheese smells so bad but tastes so good, and Matt Tebbutt heads to France to get the skinny on diet champagne(n)

programme.title: Jamie's Comfort Food Bites
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver is joined by his friend, the Italian chef Gennaro Contaldo, to make bolognese ravioli(n)

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Kate Quilton learns about a potentially life-saving property of discarded shrimp shells and Matt Tebbutt investigates why people cannot seem to get enough of salted caramel. Plus, Jimmy Doherty explores the price differences between fresh and frozen pizzas in supermarkets(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Dick Strawbridge visits Philip and Angelina and helps them build a bridge at their chateau - will Dick's design work? Jonathan and Michael test the services they provide at their chateau by inviting Michael's old college mates for the weekend, while Jonny and Ashley continue the search for their dream chateau(n)

programme.title: Bake Off: The Professionals
programme.desc: Judges Cherish Finden and Benoit Blin put the five remaining teams to the test making layered slices. The first stage of the challenge involves making 24 tiramisu and 24 roulade praline slices in three and a half hours, while for the showpiece, they must prepare enough modern-themed croquembouches to serve 36 people(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien search for more gems hidden among unwanted possessions, trying to make a profit from church organ pipes and old leather sports car seats(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contest begins at the Swan Inn in Pontfadog, a village in the Ceiriog Valley in Wrexham. Ian and Cathy McNeill take their guests trout fishing, and round the day off with a meal in their own restaurant with entertainment from a traditional tenor. However, the next morning is not so harmonious as the guests jostle for access to the only available shower(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition moves to Bournemouth, where owners John and Chris Griffith welcome their guests to the Bed & Breakfast by the Beach. The visit gets off to a bad start when hyper-critical Jill is reduced to tears by the discovery that her room does not have a smoke alarm, and the tension continues to mount at dinner, where the hosts regale their guests with racy tales(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition heads to the Lansbury in Whitby, North Yorkshire. All the guests are keen to have a good look around their rooms after falling victim to owner Jill Winspear's criticism earlier in the week - but her attempts at a guided tour backfire, and the breakfast service fails to impress(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The group's final destination is Charlie and Angie Hobson's Castle House in Denbigh, north Wales. Some of the guests are unimpressed by a day trip to a local spa - but back at the B&B, the previously critical Jill is full of praise(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The four sets of owners discover what the other competitors thought of their businesses by opening the cash-filled envelopes, having put in what they believed their hosts should receive. They meet for one last time to learn who has been declared the best value B&B of the week(n)

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Property expert Phil Spencer goes house-hunting in Hertfordshire on behalf of newlyweds Carl and Liz, who want to leave London for a new start in St Albans. Can he find them a home with space that is also well-connected for £425,000? Meanwhile, Kirstie Allsopp assists Naomi, who wants a place in Hemel Hempstead with enough room for her pole-dancing equipment(n)

programme.title: Catching History's Criminals: The Forensics Story
programme.desc: An insight into the development of forensic science as a way of catching murderers over the past 200 years, charting the scientific breakthroughs that have changed the course of justice. Surgeon and writer Gabriel Weston begins by looking at the difficulty of identifying the body in a murder case, from charred bones to bodies completely dissolved in acid. She focuses on the use of teeth and bite marks to identify a victim or killer, traces the uses of insects to pinpoint the time of death, and meets the geneticist who pioneered the technique of DNA profiling(n)

programme.title: Time Team
programme.sub-title: The House of the White Queen
programme.desc: Tony Robinson and the team head to Groby Old Hall in Leicestershire, formerly the home of Edward IV's wife Elizabeth Woodville, and discover a surprising array of archaeological remains beneath its gardens, including a medieval wall and possible evidence of a Norman castle. They are joined by author Philippa Gregory, who delves into the complex history of the Greys, the house's former owners and one of the most powerful dynasties in medieval Britain(n)

programme.title: Time Team
programme.desc: Tony Robinson and the team travel to Jersey, where they search for the origins of Mont Orgueil Castle, an imposing fortress constructed during the reign of King John. Their excavation is complicated by a newer castle that was built on top of the old foundations in Tudor times - and the entire site is located on a rocky outcrop with sides so steep they can only be scaled by mountaineers(n)

programme.sub-title: The Mystery of the Manor Moat

programme.title: Catching History's Criminals: The Forensics Story
programme.desc: Gabriel Weston explores the forensic advances that have elevated murder weapons from mere instruments to crucial pieces of evidence. She sees how arsenic, the undetectable weapon of choice in the 19th century, was exposed as the killer thanks to one simple chemical test, and finds out how distinctive marks on a victim's skull led to the weapon and, in turn, the killer. Gabriel also looks at the latest forensic developments, from 3-D laser scanning that can reconstruct the sequence of events at the scene of a gun crime to the pioneering chemistry able to determine where in the world someone has been based on just a few strands of their hair(n)

programme.title: The Hunt for Hitler's Scientists

programme.title: Time Team
programme.desc: Tony Robinson and the team investigate Gateholm Island, off the coast of Pembrokeshire, where a number of mysterious artefacts were discovered some time ago, suggesting the site was once of religious importance. The only way to get to the island is by rigging a 500-metre zip wire way above the wave-lashed rocks. The archaeologists battle inclement weather to begin a dig on the mainland, where team leader Francis Pryor sees characteristics suggestive of a classic Iron Age fort beginning to emerge(n)

programme.title: Ross Kemp: Extreme World
programme.desc: Ross Kemp visits the Central American republic of Honduras, where he makes his way to the city of San Pedro Sula. With a rate of 169 homicides per 100,000 inhabitants last year - an average of more than three people each day - Sula has the dubious distinction of being the most dangerous city on the planet outside of a war zone. Ross discovers how armed drug gangs have taken over many parts of the country, forcing thousands to flee north on a freight train that transports migrants through Guatemala and across Mexico into the United States. But it's an arduous journey and not for the faint-hearted. Even if passengers avoid falling off the roof of the train to their deaths, they face being robbed by bandits, raped, murdered or kidnapped and sold into slavery(n)

programme.title: Roman Britain from the Air
programme.desc: The Romans were the first to invade and occupy Britain and their architecture and way of life have left a legacy that has lasted 1,600 years. Christine Bleakley takes to a helicopter to view remains that are best seen from the air, while historian Michael Scott is on the ground to explore further what each sky-high vantage point reveals. Their journey takes them from London, across to Wales and north to Hadrian's Wall, visiting fascinating sites that include the best-preserved Roman legionary barracks anywhere in Europe, the 6,000-seat amphitheatre at Caerleon and town walls that stretch for more than a mile(n)

programme.title: Mud Men
programme.desc: Steve Brooker and Johnny Vaughan search for hidden treasures along the banks of the River Thames, using metal detectors and shovels to find artefacts that offer a new perspective on London's history. They begin by the Tower of London, where they investigate the military origins of a mysterious gold trumpet and reunite a 250-year-old musket ball with the gun that fired it(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: A Bournemouth estate agent searches for a dream holiday home with his grown-up daughter, taking his 40 years' experience in the UK market to La Palma in the Canaries. The island offers the pair a variety of properties to choose from, including sea-view bungalows, tailor-made condos and colonial houses(n)

programme.title: Heyday TV

programme.title: Hell is Sold Out
programme.desc: An author long assumed dead makes a surprise return, only to find books he did not write are being published under his name. He discovers that another writer, claiming to be his widow, is passing off her own creations as his previously unseen work to get them published. programme.title: Trust Me, I'm a Doctor
programme.desc: The team returns to investigate more health stories, with Chris van Tulleken examining the intriguing idea that the way we prepare food could be damaging our health. Okay, so we all know that frying isn't as good for us as steaming, but a radical new experiment suggests that the health-conscious among us could be cutting calories by making simple adjustments to the way we prepare starchy foods. in fact, certain methods could make them healthier than anyone previously imagined. Plus, surgeon Gabriel Weston witnesses a new procedure giving new hope to paralysed people in America, and journalist Michael Mosley looks at evidence that suggests we should all be spending a sensible amount of time in the sun(n)
